summary: Updated the project’s dtk-template-derived tooling to decomp-toolkit v0.9.3 and synchronized configure/build generation support with newer template features. The PR touched Nix packaging, configure.py, and tools/project.py: it bumped DTK hashes and tags, added a --non-matching build mode, added custom Ninja rule/step hooks, improved rebuild dependencies, adjusted default targets for non-matching builds, expanded objdiff scratch compiler mappings, and propagated extra_cflags into objdiff scratch config. There were no PR body notes or review comments in the provided slice, so rationale is inferred from the diff rather than reviewer discussion.

key_files.role: Bumped Nix package for decomp-toolkit from 0.7.5 to 0.9.3 and updated source/cargo dependency hashes.
Updated dtk_tag to v0.9.3, added -v short option for --version, added --non-matching config plumbing, initialized reconfig_deps, and changed one object status.

Pulled in newer dtk-template build-system features: non_matching config, reconfig_deps, custom build rules/steps, altered DOL generation flow, default target behavior, objdiff compiler mappings, and extra_cflags handling.

what_changed: Bumped decomp-toolkit version in .nix/decomp-toolkit.nix from 0.7.5 to 0.9.3.

Updated the Nix fetchFromGitHub source hash and cargoLock output hashes for the new DTK dependency graph, replacing older dol/objdiff-core hashes with nod, objdiff-core 2.0.0-alpha.3, and ppc750cl entries.
Changed configure.py tool tag config.dtk_tag from v0.7.5 to v0.9.3.
Added a short -v alias for configure.py --version while preserving choices=VERSIONS and str.upper parsing.
Added configure.py --non-matching, stored as config.non_matching, described as building equivalent but non-matching or modded objects.
Introduced Matching, NonMatching, and Equivalent naming comments in configure.py; Equivalent is tied to config.non_matching so selected objects can link only in non-matching builds.
Changed melee/ft/ftwaitanim.c from Matching to NonMatching in the object list, meaning it should not be linked in the normal matching build according to this diff.
Added ProjectConfig.reconfig_deps for extra files that should trigger regeneration of build.ninja.
Added ProjectConfig.custom_build_rules and custom_build_steps for project-specific Ninja rules and pre/post build hooks.
Added generation of custom Ninja rules and a write_custom_step helper supporting step buckets named pre-compile, post-compile, post-link, and post-build.
Inserted pre-compile outputs as implicit dependencies of link steps, post-compile outputs before link, post-link outputs before DOL generation, and post-build outputs before final check.
Moved DOL generation out of LinkStep.write and into a separate Generate DOL section after post-link custom steps.
Tracked link_outputs explicitly and used them in the check rule and, when --non-matching is active, as the default Ninja targets.
Added config.reconfig_deps to the configure rule implicit dependencies so changes to listed files cause reconfiguration.
Expanded objdiff scratch compiler mappings by enabling previously commented Metrowerks versions such as GC/3.0a3, GC/3.0a3.3, GC/3.0a3.4, GC/3.0a5, Wii/1.0RC1, Wii/1.0, and Wii/1.5.
Changed objdiff scratch c_flags generation so options['extra_cflags'] are appended when present.
Adjusted progress fancy-output wording from 'of' to 'out of' in the shown diff excerpt.
smart_moves: Kept the DTK version bump consistent across both Nix packaging and configure.py's config.dtk_tag, avoiding a split between developer shell tooling and generated project tooling.
Added --non-matching as an explicit configuration flag instead of overloading Matching/NonMatching, making equivalent or modded object linking opt-in.
Represented Equivalent as config.non_matching, which lets object lists express conditional linkability directly using the same boolean field consumed by Object.

Made non-matching builds default to link outputs rather than progress output, which fits the likely use case of building a runnable or linked artifact even when the normal matching progress/check target is not appropriate.
Added reconfig_deps as first-class ProjectConfig state and wired it into the configure rule, a reusable pattern for generated files or config fragments that must invalidate build.ninja.
Centralized custom pre/post processing through custom_build_rules and custom_build_steps rather than hardcoding project-specific asset/archive steps into the build generator.
Inserted custom steps as implicit dependencies at phase boundaries, preserving Ninja's dependency model while allowing projects to hook before compile, after compile, after link, and after build.
Propagated extra_cflags into objdiff scratch configuration, improving parity between the actual build and objdiff's scratch compile environment.
Expanded compiler version mappings instead of leaving valid toolchain versions commented out, improving objdiff support for more MWCC/MWLD variants.
decomp_lessons: When updating dtk-template or decomp-toolkit, check all version pins together: Nix package version, Nix source hash, cargoLock output hashes, and configure.py's dtk_tag.

A decomp project benefits from separate object states for matching, nonmatching, and conditionally equivalent files; this lets the normal build stay strict while still supporting modded or equivalent builds.

Build-generator extensions should be represented as generic configuration fields, such as custom_build_rules and custom_build_steps, so future project-specific steps do not fork the template logic unnecessarily.
If generated assets, scripts, or config fragments affect build generation, add them to reconfig_deps so Ninja reruns configure when they change.
Objdiff scratch settings must include the same relevant flags as the real compile; missing extra_cflags can make scratch comparisons misleading.
After DTK/tooling upgrades, audit object status changes carefully: this PR marks ftwaitanim.c NonMatching, which affects what is linked in the strict matching build.
Phase-specific dependency hooks are safer than ad hoc command ordering; model pre-compile, post-compile, post-link, and post-build artifacts as implicit dependencies of the correct downstream target.

naming_conventions.meaning: Boolean alias True; object matches and should be linked.
Boolean alias False; object does not match and should not be linked in the normal matching build.
Alias for config.non_matching; object should be linked only when configure.py is run with --non-matching.
ProjectConfig dictionary keyed by phase names such as pre-compile, post-compile, post-link, and post-build.
ProjectConfig list of extra dependency paths that should cause reconfiguration when modified.

assembly_or_matching_tactics: Introduced an opt-in --non-matching path for building equivalent or modded objects without weakening the default matching/progress workflow.
Kept NonMatching objects excluded from the normal link by default, while enabling future objects to use Equivalent for conditional linking under --non-matching.
Changed ftwaitanim.c from Matching to NonMatching in the configure object list, implying it should not currently be treated as a strict matching linked object.
Improved objdiff scratch accuracy by appending extra_cflags to c_flags in generated objdiff config.
Expanded supported scratch compiler mappings, which can help compare code built with more Metrowerks compiler versions.

review_feedback.feedback: No issue comments, review comments, reviews, or PR body text were present in the supplied context.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that use Equivalent in configure.py object lists after --non-matching was introduced.
Find PRs that add custom_build_rules or custom_build_steps for asset processing or archive rebuilding.
Find follow-up changes to ftwaitanim.c status after it was marked NonMatching in PR 1425.
Find later dtk-template or decomp-toolkit version bumps to compare required Nix and configure.py update steps.
Find objdiff config fixes involving extra_cflags or scratch compiler mappings.
